title: ipomoea aculeata var. bona-nox
intro: Ipomoea aculeata var. bona-nox, also known as cayenne morning glory or moon vine, is a climbing vine with thorny stems and trumpet-shaped flowers that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic effects. The health benefits of Ipomoea aculeata var. bona-nox include relief from pain, reduction of inflammation, and treatment of various skin conditions such as wounds, boils, and ulcers. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical application or oral consumption, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
